Title: User Registration
Preconditions: User has the TrekMate app installed on their smartphone.
Test Data: User's name, email, password, and phone number.
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app on the smartphone.
2. Tap the "Register" button.
3. Enter the required information (name, email, password, and phone number).
4. Tap the "Register" button again to submit the form.
Expected Result: The user should receive a confirmation message that their account has been created successfully.

Title: Login
Preconditions: User has a registered account on TrekMate.
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app on the smartphone.
2. Tap the "Login" button.
3. Enter the email and password.
4. Tap the "Login" button again to submit the form.
Expected Result: The user should be logged in and redirected to the main app screen.

Title: User Registration
Preconditions: The application is installed and operational on the user's device.
Test Steps:
1. Open the application for the first time.
2. The user is invited to register.
3. The user creates a new user profile.
4. The system saves the unique user code for identification within the group.
Expected Result: The user can access their account or create a new one.

Title: Profile View
Preconditions:
The user is registered and has logged in to the application.
The application is operational and the user has an active internet connection (if the profile data is stored online).
Test Steps:
1. The user selects the option to view their profile from the application interface.
2. The user is redirected to the profile page where they can see all information related to their account.
Expected Result: The user has visualized their profile information and can navigate through the various sections of the profile or modify the information as desired.

Title: Profile Update
Preconditions:
The user is registered and has an active profile.
The user has logged in to the system.
The system is operational.
Test Steps:
1. The user selects the option to update their profile from the settings menu.
2. The system displays the pre-filled profile update form with the current user information.
3. The user makes the desired changes to the form fields.
4. The user confirms the changes.
5. The system validates the new information.
6. If the information is invalid, the system displays an error message and asks the user to correct the data.
7. If the information is valid, the system updates the user's profile with the new information and confirms the update to the user.
Expected Result: The user's information is updated and saved in the system.

Title: User Login
Preconditions: The application is installed and the user has registered.
Test Steps:
1. The user starts the application for the first time.
2. The user must log in to the application using their account credentials.
3. The user enters their email address and password associated with their account.
4. The user presses the "Login" button to access the application.
Expected Result: After the user has entered their credentials and they are verified, the user is authenticated and redirected to the main screen of the application.

Title: Route Planning
Preconditions:
The user is registered and authenticated in the application.
The application has access to the user's current location.
The system is connected to the mapping service and the weather service.
Test Steps:
1. The user starts to trace a route on the map.
2. The user selects the starting point and the destination of the route on the map.
3. The system calculates the route and displays it on the map.
4. The system requests weather information along the route.
5. The weather information is displayed in correspondence of the various points of the route.
6. The user can evaluate the weather conditions to decide whether to proceed or modify the route.
Expected Result: The route and the related weather conditions are displayed on the map.

Title: Route Details
Preconditions: The user has completed a hiking trip and has saved the route in the application.
Test Steps:
1. The user selects the "View Route Details" function from the application.
2. The user chooses the route for which they want to view the details from the list of saved routes.
3. The system retrieves the detailed information related to the selected route, including the map of the route, the estimated time, the distance traveled, elevation changes, and other relevant statistics.
4. The information is presented to the user in a readable and understandable format, with the ability to zoom and navigate the map to explore specific sections of the route.
Expected Result: The user has access to all the detailed information related to the selected route and can use this information to reflect on their experience or plan future activities.

Title: Weather Information
Preconditions:
The user is registered and has logged in to the application.
The application has access to the user's current location.
The system is connected to the weather service.
Test Steps:
1. The user starts to trace a route on the map.
2. The system provides weather information for the route based on the user's current location and the selected route.
Expected Result: The user receives accurate and timely weather information that can help them make informed decisions for their safety and comfort during their hiking trip.

Title: Verify and control the position
Preconditions:
The user is registered and logged in the application.
The application has access to the user's current position.
The application is connected to a mapping service and a weather service.
Test Steps:
1. The user clicks on a specific position on the map.
2. The system shows an indicator on the selected position.
3. The system shows the local weather conditions.
Expected Result: The local weather conditions for the selected position are displayed correctly.

Title: Weather information request
Preconditions:
The user is registered and logged in the application.
The application has access to the user's current position.
Test Steps: 1. The user initiates a weather information request through the application.
Expected Result: The system acquires the user's current position and displays the local weather forecast for that area.

Title: Weather information display
Preconditions:
The user is registered and logged in the application.
The system has the user's current position and the weather forecast for that area.
Test Steps: 1. The system displays the weather forecast for the user's current position.
Expected Result: The weather forecast is displayed in a user-friendly format, with details on relevant weather phenomena (e.g. rain, snow, strong wind, etc.). The system also provides advice and recommendations based on the weather conditions.

Title: Weather information update
Preconditions:
The user is registered and logged in the application.
The system has the user's current position and the weather forecast for that area.
Test Steps:
1. During the user's hike, the system detects a significant change in the weather conditions.
2. The system notifies the user immediately.
Expected Result: The user is notified of the change in weather conditions.

Title: Alternative weather service
Preconditions: The primary weather service is unavailable.
Test Steps: 1. The system provides the user with general advice based on typical weather conditions for that area and that time of year.
Expected Result: The user receives general advice based on typical weather conditions for that area and that time of year.

Title: Access and manage past workouts
Preconditions:
The user has performed at least one workout previously.
The user has access to the system.
Test Steps:
1. The user accesses the "Health" section of the platform.
2. The system shows a list of past workouts, ordered chronologically.
3. The user can select a workout to view detailed statistics.
4. The system provides information such as duration, steps, distance covered, and calories burned.
5. The user can filter workouts based on specific parameters (e.g. date, type of workout, etc.).
Expected Result: The user has visualized, managed past workouts.

Title: Training registration and monitoring
Preconditions: The user has access to the system.
Test Steps:
1. The user starts the training feature.
2. The system starts a timer to track the duration.
3. During the training, the system records the number of steps, distance covered, and calories burned.
4. The user can interact with the system to view real-time statistics.
5. At the end of the training, the user stops the registration.
6. The system saves the statistics and updates the user's profile.
Expected Result: The statistics of the training are registered and updated in the user's profile.

Title: User identification

For administrators, it provides access to advanced and management functions.
For regular users, it is essential for personalizing the user experience, saving preferences, and participating actively in the application community.
Expected Result: The registration process is completed successfully.

Title: Communication with servers

Test Steps: 1. The application communicates with various servers to ensure constant and real-time updates of all information.
Expected Result: Both administrators and regular users always have access to the most up-to-date information.

Title: Position update
Preconditions: None

Title: Interaction and visualization of information
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Users can interact with the system to view relevant signals and information within the application.
The system calculates the distances between the signals present and the user's position, showing the results in an intuitive and easily navigable map.
Administrators, in addition, can manage and moderate these signals to maintain order and the reliability of the shared information.
Expected Result: Users can interact with the system and visualize relevant signals and information.
